sec.method: SCFA LC-MS (3-NPH derivatization) of spent medium

growth_records.cite: Weiss, Anna S. et al. In vitro interaction network of a synthetic gut bacterial community. The ISME Journal (2022). DOI:10.1038/s41396-021-01153-z. [PMC8941000]
growth_records.snippet: fast growing strains (GR > 1.5 h-1, ... C. innocuum I46 ...) ... Butyric acid is produced by strains F. plautii YL31 and C. innocuum I46, the latter also being the only strain of the consortium excreting valeric acid and caproic acid ... la
growth_records.notes: growth_rate 1.5 h-1 = lower bound of 'fast' bin (exact in Table S2, unavailable). Secretion of butyrate/valerate/caproate/lactate qualitative by SCFA LC-MS; no numeric rates. Uniquely excretes valeric + caproic acid among the 12 strains.

growth_records.cite: Pau Pérez Escriva et al. Distinct N and C Cross-Feeding Networks in a Synthetic Mouse Gut Consortium. mSystems (2022). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01484-21. [PMC9040589]
growth_records.snippet: Table 1: Clostridium innocuum I46 (Firmicutes), Max OD 1.98, Mean μ ± SD 1.34 ± 0.13.

phenotypes.base_medium: Acid/gas-production test: peptone/complex base + substrate + pH indicator — positive = fermentative acid production, NOT growth on the substrate as a sole carbon source (interpret with care for model validation)
phenotypes.citation: BacDive strain 130922 (Reimer LC et al., BacDive 2022, NAR); https://bacdive.dsmz.de/strain/130922
